raw_response: I agree with the assessment provided for Option D being the correct answer. While tongue thrusting frequently leads to spaced dentition, crowded teeth, and open bites—due to its influence on proper muscle function during swallowing—a flush terminal plane describes a condition where both the maxillary and mandibular incisors align perfectly without any protrusion or retrusion. Although rare, some individuals may have naturally aligned incisors regardless of their oral habits including tongue positioning; thus, a flush terminal plane doesn’t necessarily indicate tongue thrusting alone.
